promotion_gate.rule: A heavy backend must beat or simplify an existing heavy route on shared samples before promotion.
required_evidence: backend scorecard with external wrapper evidence

review_questions: Does the fast local route preserve headings and reading order?
Is a heavy OCR/VLM route unnecessary?

candidate_backend_previews.best_for: PDF metadata, outline, page-count, split/merge utility evidence when PyMuPDF output needs a lightweight second opinion
candidate_backend_previews.default_policy: candidate-only diagnostics; not default Markdown conversion

readiness_contract.runtime_requirement: pure Python package; no GPU or model weights
readiness_contract.manual_start_hint: No service is started by this project; use plan/fake mode until a human prepares the external runtime.
readiness_contract.ready_state: ready only after env, model/cache, and manually managed service checks pass

will_not: process private documents without explicit user input

next_actions.why: inspect command/artifact plan before any real backend execution
review normalized wrapper result before comparing quality
fold wrapper/candidate artifacts into promotion gates
compare layout/table/formula evidence before route changes

Are card/infographic layouts not forced into tables?
